content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Por-rrrcAt- Pnecrrces CoMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 25,2008
Angela lvey
City Clerk
City of Solana Beach
635 South Highway 101
Solana Beach, CA 9207 5-2215
Your Request frlr General Information
Our File No. G-08-084
Dear Ms. Ivey:
This letter is in response to yoLlr request for general information regarding the
proposed amendment to the Election Code (the "Code") of the City of Solana Beach.
You have asked us to review the proposed amendment for conformity with the
requirements of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 we have the iollowing
suggestions.
Definitions - Section 2.24.030
We recommend that you eliminate the definition of "contribution" and instead use
the Act's definition contained in Government Code Section 82015 and Regulation 18215.
Of particular concern is how Section 82015(bX2)(BXiii) provides rhat while payments
made principally for legislative, govemmental, or charitable purposes are not considered
contributions, such payments that are made at the behest of a candidate who is an elected
official (such as a city council member) must be reported to the official's agency within
30 days if the aggregate of sr.rch payments from one source reaches $5,000 in a calendar
year. This requirement is dropped from your definition. Also, there are quite a few
exceptions in Regulation 18215 that we think yor-r would want to make available to your
candidates and committees.
We also question your definition of "candidate" and subsequent use of that term
throughout the Code. The definition in subsection B-6 appears to limit that term to
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Co<.le sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Governntent Code. unless otherwise indicatecl. The regulation.s of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through llJ997 of T'itle 2 0f the Califirrnia
Ctde of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title l. Division 6 of the Califrlrnia Code rlf
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.


File No. G-08-084
Page No. 2
candidates seeking election to the city council. Once the term is so narrowly defined, its
use throughout the Code, especially in the definitions of "city committee," . primarily
formed committee," "general pllrpose committee," and "sponsored committee" is
confusing. (We realize that these definitions come from Government Code Sections
82027 .5 and 82048.7 .) It it is, indeed, yoLrr intent that all provisions of the Code dealing
with candidates are limited to candidates for city council, ihen you should review each
use of the term to be sure that its Llse conveys the intended meaning. A better approach,
we believe, is by use of the terms "city candidate" or "candidate foi city elective office,'
in various parts of the Code. You could use the Government Code Section g2OO7
definition for "candidate" and then separately define ..city candidate."
If you are limiting the operation of this code to candidates for city council, you
may want to delete the language in subsection B-(6)(iii) stating "whethei or not the
specific elective office for which he or she will seek nomination is known at the time the
contribution is received or the expenditure is made ...."
"Fundraiser event" does not need to be defined because we did not find it used
anywhere in the Code.
Expenditures - Section 2.24.050
We suggest changing subsection B to read: "City committees not controlled by
candidates that make expenditures in support of or in opposition to a candidate or
measure shall clearly .. . ."
ln subsection C, third line, we believe you should replace "person', with ,.city
committee." "Person" is very broadly defined. It would inClude, for example, a state
independent expenditure committee that supports both a local candidate and candidates or
measures being voted on in more than one county. This goes beyond the city,s
jurisdiction. The city can only regulate city committees.
In subsection E, we question the use of the word "committee" when describing
who can draw checks.
Election campaign accounts - Section 2.24.070
As written, subsection E-3 appears in conflict with Government Code Section
85201(e), which requires that all campaign expenditures shall be made from the
campaign bank account. Subsection E-3 treats some expenditures as if they had been
made from the campaign bank accoLlnt when, in fact, th-y were not. Reguiation 1g526
sets forth the circumstances under which expenditures can be reimbr-rrr.d f.o11 u
campaign bank accottnt. We recommend that you add this as an additional proviso in this
section. It would look something like this:
"For purposes of this section, any expenditures shall be deemed


File No. G-08-084
Page No. 3
made by check drawn against the election campaign account,
provided that: (i) the maker of the expenditures is reimbursed
by such check; (ii) the expenditure is reported as provided in
SBMC 2.24.080; and (iii) rhe requirements of Section 19526
of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations are met.,'
Media
-Section 2.24.I00
In subsection A-5, in the second line from the bottom, it seems that "he" should
be replaced by "it" because it refers to a "person (other than an individual)."
Enforcement -
2.24.r30
In subsection B, there's a typo in the last sentence. "council', should be
"collnsel."
If you have questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-57g3
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
VJ:jgl

sections.question_synthetic: Does the City of Solana Beach's proposed amendment to its Election Code conform to the requirements of the California Political Reform Act, and what revisions are recommended?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides detailed suggestions for revising the proposed Election Code amendment across multiple sections, including recommendations to use the Act's definitions for contributions and candidates, clarify jurisdiction limits, align with Government Code requirements regarding campaign accounts, and correct technical language and typos.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a general information request letter (File No. G-08-084), not a formal advice letter with traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The document contains substantive FPPC recommendations organized by Code section rather than Q&A format. Minor OCR issues detected in header ('Pnecrrces' for 'Practices', 'Por-rrrcAt-' for 'Political'). Confidence slightly reduced due to lack of explicit conclusion statement, but the nature and content of FPPC response is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the City of Solana Beach's proposed amendment to its Election Code conform to the requirements of the California Political Reform Act, and what revisions are recommended?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides detailed suggestions for revising the proposed Election Code amendment across multiple sections, including recommendations to use the Act's definitions for contributions and candidates, clarify jurisdiction limits, align with Government Code requirements regarding campaign accounts, and correct technical language and typos.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the City of Solana Beach's request for review of its proposed Election Code amendment by providing specific recommendations for conformity with the Political Reform Act, addressing definitional issues, jurisdictional scope, campaign account procedures, and technical corrections.
